title: Amanda Iarusso, K.C.
content:
## Amanda Iarusso, K.C.
People.ContactEmail aiarusso@mccarthy.ca
- University of Windsor
- University of Detroit Mercy
Amanda Iarusso is a senior associate in our Toronto office and a key member of the firm’s Public Law group . Amanda’s litigation practice focuses on class actions, securities litigation, and a wide range of commercial disputes. She also maintains an active advisory, helping clients navigate legal issues at the intersection of politics, public policy, and business.
Amanda has appeared before the Ontario Court of Appeal, Ontario Superior Court of Justice, and various regulatory bodies. Selected experience includes:
- Setting the standard in Canadian law to establish privilege to protect internal investigation reports by Special Committees of Boards of public issuers, in a securities class action: Vecchio Longo v. Aphria et al. , 2023 ONSC 6336.
- Defending a specialty laboratory testing services company in a national data breach class action.
- Defending a national energy company in a class action in respect of its administration of its pension plan (matter ongoing).
- Acting for a large financial institution in the defence of vacation pay class action, and successfully bringing the second-ever motion to sequence a summary judgment motion prior to certification under section 4.1 of the reformed Class Proceedings Act : Davis v. Desjardins Financial Services Firm Inc. , 2022 ONSC 2016.
- Acting for a mining company in upholding the first decision on the merits of a class action for secondary market representation under Part XXIII.1 of the Securities Act and the first of its kind in Canada. (The decision provides valuable guidance for issuers on the materiality analysis and it expands the availability of the reasonable investigation defence: Wong v. Pretium Resources Inc. , 2021 ONSC 54; aff’d 2022 ONCA 549.)
Amanda brings significant political experience to her private practice, most recently serving as the Executive Director of Policy and Legal Affairs to the Attorney General of Ontario, and previously a Legislative Aide to the Minister of Labour of Canada. Amanda has elite experience advising on novel regulatory regimes and major commercial projects with government stakeholders. She regularly advises political parties, corporations, non-profits, and executives on their legal obligations regarding conflicts of interest, election financing, political advertising, and lobbying compliance.
From 2019 to 2021, Amanda was one of the Attorney General of Ontario’s most trusted advisors. She served as a senior leader in Ontario’s justice sector, with a reputation for rapidly transforming and modernizing service delivery during the COVID-19 pandemic. Amanda had the unique role of advising the Attorney General as both a Cabinet Minister charged with strategy, policy, and procurements, and Chief Legal Officer of the Executive Council, managing litigation and legal risk for the government. Amanda played a leading role in the passage of numerous bills; namely the overhaul to the Class Proceedings Act and Election Finances Act .
Giving back to her community and hometown is very important to Amanda. She is currently an Ambassador with the Meagan Bebenek Foundation, having raised over $150,000 since 2025 in support of critical paediatric brain cancer research. She recently completed four years of service on the University of Windsor Board of Governors’ Human Resources Committee, and also spent many years as a Board Member of her local Rotary International club, focussing on clean water, women empowerment, and youth leadership projects.
In 2023, Amanda became King’s Counsel – the youngest person in Canada to ever receive this distinction. She is recognized by Best Lawyers in Canada as “One to Watch” in corporate and commercial litigation, and an “Emerging Leader (Legal)” by The Peak. Amanda has also received the 2023 Emerging Leaders in the Law Award by the University of Windsor Faculty of Law, and the 2021 Alumni Odyssey Award from the University.
